mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS rate lowers the equity discount rate and supports risk-taking, so sustained easing should lift S&P 500 returns over the next month. The signal is valid when the policy rate is already near the lower bound and inflation is not accelerating.
failure_condition: Reject if 20d SPX forward returns are not higher after monthly FEDFUNDS declines than after flat or rising rates, or if the effect disappears when CPIAUCSL inflation is stable or falling.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE above a recent trend threshold marks deteriorating labor demand and weaker household income expectations, which should depress equity returns over the next month. The effect should be strongest when payroll growth also stalls.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered UNRATE spikes are followed by positive 20d SPX returns in most cases, or if the signal vanishes when PAYEMS is flat or declining.

mechanism_narrative: A broad sales slowdown in RSAFS indicates weaker final demand and lower revenue growth expectations for cyclicals, which should translate into negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if negative or flat monthly RSAFS changes do not precede below-baseline 20d SPX returns, or if the sign flips positive in rolling out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Acceleration in INDPRO signals improving factory output and cyclical production momentum, which should support earnings revisions and positive S&P 500 returns over the next week. The effect is a short-horizon momentum response rather than a valuation channel.
failure_condition: Reject if positive 1-month changes in INDPRO do not predict positive 5d SPX returns, or if the predictive relation reverses for more than one rolling year.

mechanism_narrative: A concurrent rise in HOUST and RSAFS signals stronger housing-linked spending and retail demand, which should boost short-term equity returns through improved growth expectations. The interaction matters because either series alone is too noisy to identify a broad demand impulse.
failure_condition: Reject if joint HOUST-RSAFS strength does not outperform either proxy alone in predicting positive 20d SPX returns, or if the combined signal has no incremental information in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is high relative to recent history, financial conditions are restrictive and equity multiples compress, so S&P 500 returns should be negative over the next quarter. This signal should be strongest after persistent rate hikes rather than isolated moves.
failure_condition: Reject if high-FEDFUNDS regimes are not followed by below-median 60d SPX returns, or if the relationship fails to appear after sustained tightening episodes.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in PAYEMS and increase in UNRATE identifies a genuine labor-market deterioration, which should depress equity returns over the next month through weaker consumption and earnings expectations. The signal fails when only one labor series weakens without the other.
failure_condition: Reject if the combined PAYEMS-down/UNRATE-up pattern does not produce negative 20d SPX returns more often than chance, or if the interaction adds no value beyond each single series.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-shock recovery, rising HOUST and PAYEMS together indicate rebuilding household formation and income, which should support positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter. The mechanism is strongest when inflation is not accelerating, because real purchasing power then improves.
failure_condition: Reject if joint gains in HOUST and PAYEMS do not predict positive 60d SPX returns, or if the effect disappears when CPIAUCSL inflation is accelerating.
